methodology.description: Normalized discrepancy ledger synthesized from 4 upstream audit beads. Each finding is classified by root-cause and user-impact severity.

root_cause_taxonomy.missing_implementation: Code feature not present; requires new code to resolve
root_cause_taxonomy.alias_mismatch: Naming divergence between Pi and upstream/user expectations
root_cause_taxonomy.test_evidence_gap: Test fixture or assertion missing for claimed behavior
root_cause_taxonomy.docs_mismatch: Documentation absent or inconsistent with reality
root_cause_taxonomy.logging_schema_drift: Log/artifact schemas incomplete, inconsistent, or unenforced

severity_levels.critical: Users cannot use a provider or core feature
severity_levels.high: Users likely to hit confusion or failure in common workflows
severity_levels.medium: Users may hit issues in less common workflows
severity_levels.low: Cosmetic or minor issues unlikely to affect most users
severity_levels.info: Informational observation with no direct user impact

discrepancies.title: Naming divergence: Pi 'fireworks' vs upstream 'fireworks-ai'
discrepancies.user_impact: Users typing 'fireworks-ai' (upstream convention) resolve correctly via alias. No functional breakage.
discrepancies.remediation_note: Low-priority naming-policy decision remains intentionally deferred because 'fireworks-ai' resolves through the supported alias and canonical configs should keep using 'fireworks' until a direct migration bead proves the change. Owner path: bd-ryg6j roadmap plus bd-ryg6j.7 closeout must either keep this as an explicit permanent-alias decision or split out a migration bead once Beads create-lock health permits it.

discrepancies.title: Naming divergence: Pi 'azure-openai' vs upstream 'azure'
discrepancies.user_impact: Users typing 'azure' resolve correctly via alias. No functional breakage.
discrepancies.remediation_note: Low-priority naming-policy decision remains intentionally deferred because 'azure' resolves through the supported alias and canonical configs should keep using 'azure-openai' until a direct migration bead proves the change. Owner path: bd-ryg6j roadmap plus bd-ryg6j.7 closeout must either keep this as an explicit permanent-alias decision or split out a migration bead once Beads create-lock health permits it.

discrepancies.title: Common provider aliases remain partially complete
discrepancies.user_impact: Users typing common provider names (together, grok, hf, nim, pplx, etc.) got 'provider not found' errors despite the provider being fully implemented.
discrepancies.evidence: bd-3uqg.14.3.4 comments: Gap 1, src/provider_metadata.rs alias fields, docs/provider-auth-troubleshooting.md provider name crosswalk
discrepancies.remediation_note: Provider docs now report 88 canonical providers and 41 aliases, and src/provider_metadata.rs covers the high-traffic UX aliases from bd-tuh3g plus later aliases such as nanogpt, novita, zhipu, glm, lm-studio, vercel-ai-gateway, copilot, gitlab-duo, and Azure variants. Remaining work is a targeted review of no-alias/under-aliased longtail providers, tracked by bd-ryg6j.3.

discrepancies.title: No --list-providers CLI command
discrepancies.user_impact: Users had no way to discover available providers, their aliases, or required auth env keys from the CLI.
discrepancies.remediation_note: Implemented in bd-3gsk0 (CopperBrook). Displays table of all 88 providers with canonical ID, aliases, auth env keys, and API type.

discrepancies.title: Model selector did not search provider aliases
discrepancies.user_impact: Users typing alias names (e.g., 'grok', 'together', 'hf') in the interactive model selector got no results, even though the provider existed.
discrepancies.evidence: src/model_selector.rs:matches_query() only searched canonical provider ID
discrepancies.remediation_note: Implemented in bd-1gi21 (CopperBrook). matches_query() now checks provider_metadata() aliases.

discrepancies.title: Generic 'Provider not implemented' error with no suggestions
discrepancies.user_impact: Users who mistyped a provider name got an unhelpful error with no guidance on similar valid providers.
discrepancies.remediation_note: Implemented in bd-1fgjj (CopperBrook). suggest_similar_providers() provides 'Did you mean: X?' suggestions using prefix/substring matching.

discrepancies.title: No display_name field on ProviderMetadata
discrepancies.user_impact: Users see cryptic IDs like '302ai', 'xai', 'moonshotai' in model listings and selector instead of human-friendly names like 'Together AI', 'Grok (xAI)'.
discrepancies.evidence: src/provider_metadata.rs: ProviderMetadata includes display_name and PROVIDER_METADATA populates it per provider.
discrepancies.remediation_note: ProviderMetadata now includes display_name and the catalog populates it for all canonical providers.

discrepancies.title: First-time setup only recognizes 3 providers
discrepancies.user_impact: Interactive onboarding only offers Anthropic, OpenAI, Google. Users of other providers (Groq, Cohere, DeepSeek, etc.) get no setup guidance.
discrepancies.evidence: src/main.rs: PROVIDER_CHOICES expanded and provider_choice_from_token accepts canonical IDs/aliases.
discrepancies.remediation_note: Onboarding choices now include additional providers (Cohere, Groq, DeepSeek, Mistral, OpenRouter, Azure) and fall back to provider metadata when users type other canonical IDs or aliases.

discrepancies.title: Bedrock missing 2 error-scenario VCR cassettes
discrepancies.user_impact: No direct user impact. Error handling paths for Bedrock are untested by VCR replay, but are covered by unit-level assertion tests.
discrepancies.evidence: bd-3uqg.14.3.2 comments: Deviation 1. AWS uses different error signaling format.
discrepancies.remediation_note: Owned by bd-ryg6j.6. Add credential-free deterministic Bedrock auth/rate-limit error coverage where possible; if true VCR cassettes require AWS credentials, keep that blocker explicit and link unit/request-shape proof.

discrepancies.title: GitLab missing tool_call VCR cassette
discrepancies.user_impact: No direct user impact. GitLab API doesn't support standard tool-call format, so this is an API limitation, not a missing test.
discrepancies.remediation_note: GitLab's proprietary Chat API remains non-standard for tool calls; src/providers/gitlab.rs now has a negative request-encoding test proving available ToolDef schemas are omitted instead of serialized into unsupported tools/tool_choice/functions fields.

discrepancies.title: 10 provider_factory tests fail due to missing pi_runtime.json VCR cassette
discrepancies.user_impact: No direct user impact. Infrastructure issue in test harness, not a provider behavior gap.
discrepancies.remediation_note: Owned by bd-ryg6j.5. Reproduce current provider_factory behavior and either add deterministic fixture/config handling or close this as stale with focused command evidence.

discrepancies.title: CI correlation ID not propagated to JSONL records
discrepancies.user_impact: Cross-CI-run correlation requires manual matching. Does not affect test execution or provider behavior.
discrepancies.evidence: tests/common/logging.rs: TestLogger reads CI_CORRELATION_ID and includes ci_correlation_id; validators accept the field.
discrepancies.remediation_note: pi.test.log.v2 now supports ci_correlation_id, and TestLogger injects CI_CORRELATION_ID into every JSONL record.

discrepancies.title: No per-provider artifact redaction scan
discrepancies.user_impact: Potential for unredacted API keys in provider e2e output artifacts. Redaction functions exist and are unit-tested but are not applied to actual provider output.
discrepancies.evidence: tests/e2e_live_harness.rs: assert_json_values_redacted runs find_unredacted_keys over live provider logs/results/artifacts.
discrepancies.remediation_note: Live provider harness and redaction scans now enforce find_unredacted_keys() over JSONL artifacts.
affected_providers: all providers with live test runs

discrepancies.title: Artifact-index paths not cross-validated
discrepancies.user_impact: Artifact-index.jsonl may reference paths that don't exist. No direct user impact (internal tooling).
discrepancies.remediation_note: Artifact index validation now cross-checks referenced paths and reports missing entries.

discrepancies.title: No formal evidence_contract JSON schema
discrepancies.user_impact: No direct user impact. evidence_contract.json referenced in CI policy but lacks formal schema definition.
discrepancies.remediation_note: Formal evidence_contract JSON schema added as docs/evidence-contract-schema.json.

discrepancies.title: V1 log schema deprecation not enforced
discrepancies.user_impact: No direct user impact. New tests could use deprecated v1 schema without warning.
discrepancies.evidence: tests/common/logging.rs: validate_jsonl_v2_only rejects pi.test.log.v1; used in tests/validate_e2e_artifact_schema.rs.
discrepancies.remediation_note: V2-only JSONL validation added and used in schema validation to reject new v1 log records.

discrepancies.title: No provider-name crosswalk documentation
discrepancies.user_impact: Users have no reference table showing canonical IDs, aliases, common brand names, and auth env keys in one place.
discrepancies.evidence: docs/providers.md canonical provider matrix and alias migration table; docs/provider-auth-troubleshooting.md provider name crosswalk with canonical IDs, aliases, auth env vars, and endpoints
discrepancies.remediation_note: --list-providers remains the runtime discovery surface, and static docs now provide the requested provider-name crosswalk. docs/providers.md records source-of-truth references and docs/provider-auth-troubleshooting.md reports 88 canonical providers, 41 aliases, and env-key/endpoint mappings.

discrepancies.title: CLI --provider help text only mentions 3 providers
discrepancies.user_impact: Users reading --help see 'e.g., anthropic, openai, google' and may not realize 85+ other providers are available.
discrepancies.evidence: src/cli.rs: --provider help text points to --list-providers and mentions canonical IDs/aliases.
discrepancies.remediation_note: --provider help text now points to --list-providers and mentions canonical IDs/aliases.

discrepancies.title: --list-models only shows providers with API keys set
discrepancies.user_impact: Users see only configured providers in --list-models, which is intentional but may confuse users who expect to see all available providers.
discrepancies.evidence: src/main.rs: maybe_print_list_models_note prints a subset warning and points to --list-providers.
discrepancies.remediation_note: --list-models now prints a note when showing a subset of providers and points to --list-providers for the full list.

priority_matrix.description: Discrepancies grouped by remediation priority for downstream bead creation

p1_high_value.description: High user impact, actionable code changes

cross_references.downstream_action: Materialize remediation beads for all open P1/P2 discrepancies
